narrative.intro: Through FY2026 to date federal obligations total $369B. The largest NAICS markets are led by Commercial and Institutional Building Construction at $39.7B, followed by Aircraft Manufacturing at $31.6B, Facilities Support Services at $30.3B, and Engineering Services at $30.1B. Other major categories include Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers ($27B), Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology) ($25.4B), Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing ($23.1B), Computer Systems Design Services ($18.5B), Ship Building and Repairing ($15.2B), and Other Computer Related Services ($13.1B). Total award values reported are full contract values, not single-year obligations. The list shows a mix of construction, manufacturing, services, R&D, and health-related NAICS codes occupying the top slots, indicating the government is funding large projects across physical infrastructure, aerospace and defense manufacturing, technical services, and health insurance and research domains. These figures reflect where contract value is concentrated in this reporting period and provide a snapshot of federal procurement priorities to date in FY2026.

sections.heading: Which NAICS sectors account for the largest federal contract obligations?
sections.body: Commercial and Institutional Building Construction leads the ranking at $39.7B, with Aircraft Manufacturing at $31.6B and Facilities Support Services at $30.3B. Engineering Services is close behind at $30.1B, while Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers register $27B. The top ten also include major R&D, space and systems design, shipbuilding, and computer services categories.

sections.heading: What does the mix of top NAICS codes reveal about federal contracting?
sections.body: The top NAICS codes combine construction, manufacturing, professional services, health insurance, and research and development, reflecting diverse procurement needs. High values in Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing ($23.1B) and Aircraft Manufacturing ($31.6B) show large manufacturing and defense-related contracts, while Computer Systems Design Services ($18.5B) and Other Computer Related Services ($13.1B) indicate significant investment in IT and systems work.

faq.q: What are the biggest federal contracting industries?
faq.a: The largest federal contracting industries are Commercial and Institutional Building Construction ($39.7B), Aircraft Manufacturing ($31.6B), and Facilities Support Services ($30.3B). These are followed by Engineering Services ($30.1B), Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers ($27B), and Research and Development in the Physical, Engineering, and Life Sciences (except Nanotechnology and Biotechnology) ($25.4B), with additional top categories including Guided Missile and Space Vehicle Manufacturing ($23.1B), Computer Systems Design Services ($18.5B), Ship Building and Repairing ($15.2B), and Other Computer Related Services ($13.1B).
